runs 42.1 miles (67.8 km) through the [Upper Peninsula](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula "Upper Peninsula") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan") and empties into [Lake Superior](/wiki/Lake_Superior "Lake Superior").[[1]](#cite_note-NHD-1) Along its course, it flows through the [Ottawa National Forest](/wiki/Ottawa_National_Forest "Ottawa National Forest") and [Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park](/wiki/Porcupine_Mountains_Wilderness_State_Park "Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park"). It has 300 square miles (780 km2) of drainage area in Upper Michigan and northern [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin").
U.S. Geological Survey. National Hydrography Dataset high-resolution flowline data. [The National Map](https://viewer.nationalmap.gov/viewer/), accessed May 1, 2012

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [46°42′38″N 89°58′28″W﻿ / ﻿46.71056°N 89.97444°W﻿ / 46.71056; -89.97444](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Presque_Isle_River&params=46_42_38_N_89_58_28_W_type:river_region:US-MI_source:GNIS)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

This article is about a Michigan river. For other areas, see [Presque Isle](/wiki/Presque_Isle_(disambiguation) "Presque Isle (disambiguation)").

River in Michigan, United States

| Presque Isle River | |
| --- | --- |
| Presque Isle River | |
| Map | |
| Location | |
| Country | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| State | [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan") |
|  | |
| [National Wild and Scenic River](/wiki/National_Wild_and_Scenic_Rivers_System "National Wild and Scenic Rivers System") | |
| Type | Scenic, Recreational |
| Designated | March 3, 1992 |
|  | |
|  | |

The **Presque Isle River** runs 42.1 miles (67.8 km) through the [Upper Peninsula](/wiki/Upper_Peninsula "Upper Peninsula") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan") and empties into [Lake Superior](/wiki/Lake_Superior "Lake Superior").[[1]](#cite_note-NHD-1) Along its course, it flows through the [Ottawa National Forest](/wiki/Ottawa_National_Forest "Ottawa National Forest") and [Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park](/wiki/Porcupine_Mountains_Wilderness_State_Park "Porcupine Mountains Wilderness State Park"). It has 300 square miles (780 km2) of drainage area in Upper Michigan and northern [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin").

Several waterfalls are located on the river. They include (from west to east):

* [Manabezho Falls](/wiki/Manabezho_Falls "Manabezho Falls")
* [Manido Falls](/wiki/Manido_Falls "Manido Falls")
* [Nawadaha Falls](/wiki/Nawadaha_Falls "Nawadaha Falls")
* [Iagoo Falls](/w/index.php?title=Iagoo_Falls&action=edit&redlink=1 "Iagoo Falls (page does not exist)")
* [Lepisto Falls](/w/index.php?title=Lepisto_Falls&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lepisto Falls (page does not exist)")
* [Nokomis Falls](/w/index.php?title=Nokomis_Falls&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nokomis Falls (page does not exist)")
* [Nimikon Falls](/w/index.php?title=Nimikon_Falls&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nimikon Falls (page does not exist)")
* [Minnewawa Falls](/w/index.php?title=Minnewawa_Falls&action=edit&redlink=1 "Minnewawa Falls (page does not exist)")
* [Yondota Falls](/w/index.php?title=Yondota_Falls&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yondota Falls (page does not exist)")
